column1: 6245039,"Call on 08/15/17 - Bill wording print changes, LEAC to Fuel Adjustment Charge","[2024-04-17 09:13
column2: Chris Holloway] Issues The issues described in the ticket are: The LEAC charges on bills were not being printed as the Fuel Adjustment Charge (FAC) as requested. Instead, the system was printing the FAC charges individually as FAC1, FAC3, and FAC5. Troubleshooting The following troubleshooting steps were taken: Attempted to update the wording in the admin settings under rate codes per previous instructions, but this did not resolve the issue. The developer responsible for bill form changes tested resolutions. Identified the problem over the weekend and corrected it. Next Steps No next steps were provided in the ticket conversation."

Kathy Romero] EAWSD is in the process of updating Routing information (Book/Sequence). We have done this in the past using parcel number, what information do you need from us and in what format?

Chris Holloway] HI Kathy, To recap what we looked at today, you will be rerouting and resequencing your reading books for a set of service locations. since your parcel numbers are unique in the system, if you can provide a .csv file with the parcel number, the book number and the sequence number that the meter is going to in separate fields, we should be able to create a script to re-sequence your meters. please let us know how many service locations will be included in the re-sequencing. a rough estimate should help with the quote process. I will send this request over to our professional services department to get the quote process started.

Shaena Robertson] Anthony, Good morning. Development has completed the work on the rerouting. Here are some items that came out of the development and what we will need to look at when doing in test. File given only has CR not CR+LF at end of lines so compensated. If they change will affect the processing. After looking at the file I used columns 1, 3 and 4 for account number, book, and sequence. I did not use the location in the file as that rarely gives great matching. The account numbers in the file have had their leading zeroes stripped, so I had to do a numerical equivalence to match customer records (ie convert 000031 in DB to a literal 31 before comparing to file). This probably will not be a problem unless they have varying account number lengths and have accounts that have the same value with different leading zeroes (ex 00031 and 00031 are two different account in our system but numerically it is the same so a single value in the file may match more than one customer in system). MANY of the records in the file are for customers who are not at a location in the DB we have here. I am not sure how old the data is here, so we will use your database copy live to test and then test out this process with up to date data. I return a list of accounts not found, a list of accounts that do not have a service location, and a list of accounts with service locations without the group. After it updates the book/seq on the account, it checks the books affected and if any duplicates, will resequence them. We are ready to deliver this in test. Do you have new file to try out? If not you will need to let us know when you have a file and we can schedule to copy live to test and run on the test environment. Please let us know at your earliest convenience.
